def: s.m. Causing to run over, spilling; — an affection of the eye causing it to run; a blear-eye; — adj. Blear-eyed.
v. a. See ḍhalak'nā , v. n.
v.t. To cause to run over or down, to cause to flow; to spill; to pour out; to tilt, overturn; to cause to roll, to roll ( = ḍhulkānā)
more: (caus. of ڐهلکنا) v. a. To tilt, to overturn, to spill, to pour, to roll. ¶ ḍhulkānā, v. a. (dakh.) To incite, instigate.

def: s.m. A lump of clay, a clod.

ety: by redupl. of rt. ḍhal of ḍhalnā
def: adj. Shaking, tottering, staggering, unsteady, wavering, vacillating (cf. ḍagmag).
v. n. 1. To stagger; totter; to be unsteady. 2. To vacillate; waver! change. ḍhilmil yaqīn, ḍhilmul yaqīn , adj. Wavering; fluctuating; vacillating; fickle; uncertain.
v.n. To move from side to side, to sway, totter, stagger, reel, rock; to be unsteady, to waver, vacillate (cf. ḍagmagānā).
more: v. n. To move from side to side, to totter.

def: v. a. 1. To throw; cast. 2. To pour out; tilt; spill. 1. Kachchā maṅgā le, Rājā kī beṭī, bholuā, aur kachchā maṅgā le sūt, Ek sau ek ber ḍhāl de, tab pā nī pīve asal Rājpūt . Song. Ḍholā . 3. To cast (metal in fusion); mould; form; figure; shape; coin. 4. To compose (a sentence). A chchhā mazmūn ḍhālā . 5. To speak at one ( jhukānā , 5.). ḍhāl'yā, ḍhalaiyā , n. m. One who works in melting metal and moulding it into vessels and ornaments.

v.n. To be poured out or down; to be cast (as metal), be moulded; to run, run down, flow, drop or fall (from, -se); to spill; to roll; to incline ( = ḍhulnā); to decline, sink, fade (as day, or life, &c.)
more: v. n. 1. To be cast (a metal), to be poured out, to flow, to spill. 2. To roll. 3. To incline, to decline. دن ڐهلنا din ḍhalnā, To decline the day (towards evening). ڐهلتي پهرتي چهانو ḍhaltī phirtī chhāṅw (lit. rolling and turning shade) is applied, 1. to the changeable state of worldly concerns, 2. ( met. ) to a person of a capricious or unsteady temper.
subs: v.n. (intens.), To run down, flow down, drop or fall down, &c.

def: v.t. To pour out, shed, spill, tilt; to throw, cast; to cast (metal in fusion), to mould, fashion, form, shape, coin (money), frame or compose (a sentence, &c.); to aim or direct (speech, at), to speak (at, -par); (fig.) to do mischief (to), to injure, mar, spoil.
more: v. a. 1. To cast (metal in fusion). 2. To pour out, to tilt, to spill. 3. ( met. ) To do mischief, to mar, to spoil.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Rolling; falling; moving, slipping, shifting, &c.
subs: lit. 'Shifting and moving shadow'; the changeable state of worldly concerns, the vicissitudes of life or fortune; (fig.) a person of capricious or unsteady temper.

def: adj. & s.m. Sloping, slant ( = ḍhālwāṅ, ḍhalwāṅ); — casting, moulding; — doing mischief, spoiling; — caster, moulder, fashioner, &c.; spoiler; destroyer; — slope, descent (of a hill, &c.)
more: adj. 1. Slant, sloping. 2. part. act. (of ڐهالنا) Spoiling, doing mischief, casting, &c. ڐهالوبهاڙکرنا ḍhālū bhāṛ karnā, To shift goods, &c. occasionally over shallows, &c.

def: adj. 1˚ = next, q.v.; — 2˚ Cast (as metal, i.q. ḍhalā).

def: adj. (f. -wīṅ), Sloping, slanting, declivous ( = ḍhalwāṅ); — cast, moulded ( = ḍhālā).

ety: ḍhal(nā) or ḍhul(nā)+Prk. अअं (with w inserted) = S. अ+कं
def: adj. (f. -wīṅ), Inclined, slant, sloping, declivous
adj. Sloping; slant; declivitous; descending. ḍhalāī , n. f. The cost of moulding. ḍhalvāṅ khet ; A. satah māil . ( Phys .) An inclined plane. ḍhalai'yā, ḍhalait , n. m. One who casts or moulds.
Caus. of دَهلوانا q. v.
more: v. a. Caus. of دَهونا q. v.

def: v.t. To cause to be poured out, to have or get poured out; — to cause to be cast or moulded, to have or get cast, &c.
s.f. Sound, loud noise or report (as of a drum, &c.); thud
more: n. f. S. धम to sound. A thud. dham se , adv. 1. Plump; heavily. 2. Suddenly; unexpectedly.
subs: s.f. Noise, tumult, bustle, riot, row
s.m.(f.?), Sound of walking over a hollow place, or of stamping, or of continued beating or thumping, or drumming, &c.; — adv. With a loud noise, with a bang, thump-thump, drum-drum, &c.
s.f. Sound of a drum, &c.; — 2˚ = dham-ā-dham
adv. With a loud noise or report, with a thud, noisily; heavily; suddenly, all at once.

def: adj. & s.m. The double of any number or quantity together with its half (e.g. 57½ as 2½ times 23), 2½ times any number; — the multiplication table of 2½ times.
s.m. A basket (made of cane).
more: n. m. A basket made of cane.

def: n. f. Noise; row; tumult; riot. Kyā dhamā-chaukṛi machāī hai, Terī bakhtāvari kuchh āī hai ? Wom. Shauq.
s. f. Noise, tumult, bustle.
s.m. Dwelling-place, house, abode, home, residence, place; paradise, heaven; splendour, &c. (see dhāman); name of a class of superhuman beings
more: Eng. home, n. m. S. धमन् abode, हिम snow. Place; dwelling; house; head-quarters; centre. Chhal bal kar-ke, āj maiṅ kar lāī apnā kām, Bahut khusī dil meṅ kuī, maiṅ āī apne dhām . chaubolā.
s. m. A dwelling, a house, a place.
subs: vulg. dhāmad, adj. & s.m. Heaven-giving; — a person by whom, or the means by which, paradise is obtained
dhāma-vant, dhām-vān, adj. Splendid, luminous; eminent, exalted, illustrious, heroic.

def: adj. & s.m. The double of any number or quantity together with its half (e.g. 57½ as 2½ times 23), 2½ times any number; — the multiplication table of 2½ times.
s.f. = H دهمادهم धमधम dhamā-dham, s.m. See s.v. dham.
s.m. = H دهماچوکڙي धमाचौकड़ीdhāmā-ćaukṛī, s.f. See s.v. dham.
more: n. f. The sounds made by a succession of blows in beating or stamping with the feet. Dhamādham kūdā .
s. m. The sound of stamping, thumping.

def: s.m. (f. -ikā), A blacksmith.

s.f. Noise of footsteps overhead, or such as arises from the fall of a body, sound of thumping, drumming, &c.; — shooting pain, beating, throbbing, throb, pulsation; glow, blast, hot blast; lustre, radiance, glitter; — threatening, threat; awe; — fall, or unevenness (in ground, such as would produce a shock; e.g., in kahār's idiom, dhamak hai).
more: n. f. dhamkā , n. m. 1. The sound of footsteps overhead, or of thumping, etc. 2. Throbbing. Sir meṅ dhamak ho rahī hai .
(from دهمکنا) s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. Noise of footsteps over-head, thumping, &c. 2. Threat, threatening, awe.

def: s.m. A loud report (as of a falling wall, or a gun, &c.), crack, crash, bang; — a hard blow or thump; — a kind of cannon (carried on an elephant); a firelock.
more: n. m. 1. A report of a gun; bang; thud; crash. 2. A blow; thump. 3. A firelock.
s. m. A kind of cannon carried on an elephant.

def: explosion, boom

ety: dhamak˚ = Prk. धमक्क(इ), or धमक्के(इ), fr. S. धम+कृ
def: v.n. To shoot with pain, to throb; to beat, palpitate, thump; to glow; to flash, glimmer; — to make a rush; to make a hasty inroad or incursion; to run (against); cf. dhar-dhamaknā (in this last sense dhamaknā would seem to point to S. dhāv as its source)
more: v. n. 1. To throb; shoot (as pain). 2. To make a raid; to run against. ān dhamaknā , v. n. To reach quickly. dhar dhamaknā , v. n. To start; set out.
v. n. 1. To throb, to shoot (as, the pain of a headach). 2. To palpitate, to thump. 3. To flash, to glimmer.
subs: v.n. To throb, &c.; — to resound, ring again.

def: s.m. A qalandar or calendar who leaps into, or runs through, fire.
s.m. Dwelling, house, &c. ( = dhām, q.v.); — a troop, host; — state, dignity, majesty, glory; light, splendour, radiance, brilliancy.
more: n. m. S. धावन. 1. A long snake said to suck the milk from cows. 2. A bamboo of which bows are made; grewia tiliœfolia . 3. A grass of good quality, found in the Bhaṭṭī territory. (?)
s. m. 1. A kind of serpent (which is said to suck cows and to be harmless). 2. A kind of wood. 3. A kind of bamboo.

def: s.m. A large species of snake (which is said to suck cows, and to be harmless); — a kind of wood; a kind of bamboo, Grewia tiliœfolia (of which bows are made); — dhāman, A grass of good quality (found in the Bhaṭṭī territory).
s.f. A small tube through which to blow a fire; any tubular vessel of the body, as an artery, a vein, or a nerve.
s.f. Any tubular vessel of the body; a vein.
more: n. f. A vein; any tubular vessel of the body. (?)

def: s.m. = dhamāl, q.v.
more: s. m. 1. Name of a chime or time in musick. 2. The same as دهمال dhammāl.

def: adj. See s.v. dhām. s.f. Sound, loud noise or report (as of a drum, &c.); thud
subs: s.f. Noise, tumult, bustle, riot, row
s.m.(f.?), Sound of walking over a hollow place, or of stamping, or of continued beating or thumping, or drumming, &c.; — adv. With a loud noise, with a bang, thump-thump, drum-drum, &c.
s.f. Sound of a drum, &c.; — 2˚ = dham-ā-dham
adv. With a loud noise or report, with a thud, noisily; heavily; suddenly, all at once.

def: s.f. Stamping overhead; drumming noise or sound; thumping, banging.
v.n. To produce a noise by running over a hollow place, to make a noise by stamping, &c.; to stamp repeatedly; to drum; to thump.
more: v. a. 1. To make a noise with stamping. 2. To slap; thump.
(from دهمادهم) v. n. To make a noise (with the feet, by running about over-head).

def: adj. Fat, corpulent ( = dhumpāl).
s.f. dim. of dhāmā, q.v.; — also = dhāmin, q.v.
s.f. A blacksmith's wife.
(pl. of dhāmī?), prop. n. Name of a sect, followers of Prānāth, a Hindū reformer who flourished in the seventeenth century in Bunḍelkhanḍ.
s.m. Sound produced by the fall of a heavy body; a shock; a thump, blow, buffet; — hot blast; closeness and heat (as after rain); feverishness; — threatening, chiding, threat, menace.
more: s. m. 1. Great heat. 2. Threatening, chiding. 3. Thump, noise produced by the fall of any heavy body.

def: s.f. Threatening, &c. ( = dhamkī, q.v.). s.f. Threatening, threat, menace, reprimand, snubbing
subs: (-kī), To be frightened or coerced by the threats (of another).

def: v.t. To chide, scold, threaten, menace, snub, cow, daunt; to repress by threats, or reproof, &c.
more: v. a. To threaten; menace; shake one's fist at. 1. Kisī banye ko dhamkāiyegā! 2. Saukan terī dinoṅ kamāve, tauṅ ghar baiṭhī khāve sai, Baiṭhī ṭīkaṛ pāṛe ghar māṅ, aur hamāṅ dhamkāve sai! Rus. Your co-wife works all day, you sit at home and eat, You sit and munch all day and rate and me browbeat!
v. a. To threaten, menace, chide, snub, cow, daunt.

def: s.f. Threatening, threat, menace, reprimand, snubbing
more: n. f. Threat; menace. dhamkī meṅ ānā , v. n. To be frightened or coerced by one's threats. Maiṅ āpkī dhamkī meṅ nahiṅ ānekā. nuqsān pahauṅchāne kī dhamkī denā , G. G. To hold out a threat of injury.
subs: (-kī), To be frightened or coerced by the threats (of another).

def: to threaten
adj. & s.m. Glowing; bright, glittering; — a bright colour.
v.n. To roll, totter, stagger, reel, &c. ( = ḍhalmalānā, and ḍagarnā).

s.m. Jumping into, or running through fire (a practice of faqīrs or qalandars; cf. dam-madār); skipping and capering (of qalandars, &c.); — a musical measure; a piece composed in that measure; a kind of song (sung during the Holī festival); wild and tumultuous merriment, noise, tumult, uproar; — adj. (dialec.), Bulky, big, fat, corpulent ( = dhumpāl, q.v.).
more: n. m. 1. (Mah.) Leaping into fire. 2. Noise; uproar. 3. A kind of music. 4. A kind of song sung during the saturnalia of the Holī 5. Jest; joke.
s. m. Running through fire on religious occasions (among Musalmān faḳīrs ). ¶ dhamāl, s. m. 1. See دهمار dhamār, sign. 1. 2. Kind of song which is sung during the holī.

def: s.m. A pavior's monkey, a beetle, heavy rammer or beater (with a vertical handle)
subs: v.t. To ram or beat down (a terrace, or floor, or new-laid turf).

ety: dham˚ = Prk. धम(इ) = S. धम(ति), rt. ध्मा
def: v.n. To be grieved; to be amazed or astounded.
s.m. A kind of tambourine.
more: s. m. A kind of tambourin.

def: s.m. corr. of ḍhap, q.v. s.f. The distance a man can run without taking breath, a distance of about half a mile; distance, space, range ( = dhappā); a large expanse of low ground; a ghāṭ or passage; a foot (measure of length).
s.m. A thump, blow, buffet.
s.m. The rice plant; rice in the husk; a sort of fire-work (rice husks filled with powder and attached to a small stick)
more: n. m. S. धान्य, , Pālī dhāṅnam . 1. The rice plant; rice in the husk. (Rice sown broad-cast as distinguished from jaṛ- han transplanted rice). 1. Dhān, pān, pānī, Kātak savād /?/ānī . Prov. Rice, pān , and water taste best in October. 2. Baiṭhā banyā kyā kare? is koṭhī ke dhān us meṅ . Prov. What should the Bania sitting idle do? the grain from one house shift the other to. dhān pān , adj. lit . the rice plant and the betel leaf. 1. Thin; lean; skin and bone. Phr. Thin as a wafer; thin as a shadow. 2. Slight; slim; delicate; tender. Choṭi kā bojh, ūhī! uṭhāë jo ye kamar, Būtā nahiṅ hai itnā bhī mujh dhān pān meṅ . Wom. Jān. Sāhab. To lift my long black hair, oh! it is such a weight! It breaks my back, I am so slim and delicate. dhān kī khīleṅ , n. f. Swollen parched rice. dhan-kuṭṭā, dhān-vāyā, dhanaiyā , n. m. A thresher of rice. dhan-kaṭṭī , n. f. Reaping the rice crop. dhan-kuṭṭī , n.…
s. m. The rice-plant, or rice before it is separated from the husk.
s. A sort of firework, rice husks filled with powder, with a small staff attached to them ( Qanoon-e-islam ). دهان کي کهيليان dhān ke khīliyāṅ (dakh.) Swollen parched rice ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: s.m. lit. 'Rice-leaf'; (fig.), a slender or delicate object or creature; — adj. Slender, slim, thin, delicate
s.f. Rice-cutting, reaping the rice crop
s.m. A pounder or thresher of rice
s.f. Pounding rice to separate it from the husk, threshing rice

def: s.m. Property (of any description), chattels, substance, wealth, treasure, riches, money, gold, wealth in cattle or herds, cattle; fortune, good fortune, prosperity (in this sense prob. fr. S. dhanya)
more: n. m. S. धन, Comp. Lat. pecunia . 1. Cattle; property in cattle. 2. Fortune; prosperity; riches ( daulat ). 1. Beṭī kā nimānā dhan hai . Prov. Saying. A modest daughter is a treasure. 2. Pat khove pat na mile, dhan roë nā hoë, Tulsī! karam ke anchharā kamtī baṛhtī nā hoë . Dohā. Lost honor's never found again, nor wealth with tears back brought, Tulsī! what Fate hath writ you cannot alter e'en a jot. 3. Beṭī kā dhan nikammā hai, āte bhī rulāë, jāte bhī rulāë . Prov. Saying. A worthless portion daughters are, their coming makes you weep and when they go you weep. 3. A sign of the zodiac; Sagitarius. dhan, dhan dhan, dhan hai, dhan bhāg, dhan nasīb , intj. S. धन्य 1. Blessings on you! God be praised! 2. Bravo! well done! 1. Dhan julāhan terā hīyā! Jītā khasam dhartī meṅ dīyā! Mazāq . Jest. O weaver's wife! your breast such pity swayed! Your living husband in the earth you've laid! (A jest …
s. m. 1. A bow. 2. (or dhan ) The sign Sagittarius. ¶ दहन dahan, s. f. Sultriness.

1. or dhani or dhane or dhanya (for धन्य ) adj. Fortunate; (as an interj.) well done! دهن ماننا dhan mānnā, To thank. 2. ( धन ) s. m. An inventory of property. دهنمان dhan-mān, or دهن وان धनवान् dhan-wān, adj. Affluent, rich, wealthy.

ety: dhā˚ = Prk. धा(इ) = S. धाव(ति), rt. धाव्
def: v.n. To run, go quickly, make haste, to rush; to bustle about, to toil, labour, drudge.
more: v. n. S. धावन from धाव to run; Pr. dhai, dhāu, dhāū . 1. To run; make haste; go; depart; set out. dhāo re nauvā, dhāo re bariyā, dhāo re bamhanvā ke pūt, Āveṅ Kaikaī, palaṅg chaḍh baiṭhī, harī gaī aṅgūliyā ki pīr! Rām samāj? Janakpur. Run, barber, run! leaf-platter-maker run! run Brahman's son! Come but Kekaī, my bed sit on, my finger-ache is gone! 2. To toil and labour; drudge. Dhāo dhāo! Karam kā likhā so pāo! Prov. Toil and sweat, what's in your Fate you'll get. 3. To worship; adore. Dhāvegā so pāvegā . Prov. Who worships gets. Or, who runs gets.
v. n. 1. ( धावन ) To run, to make haste; to toil and labour, to drudge. 2. (from ध्यान ) To worship.

def: v.t. To worship, adore; to invoke.

dhāṛ mārnā, To plunder in bands.

def: s.m. A heap (of corn, &c.).

s.f. A continuous line, a line, lineament; a line or chain (of mountains, &c.); edge (of a sword, &c.); sharpness; — flowing, flow, stream, current; a line or stream of descending fluid (as milk from a cow, &c.; hence), a stream of milk, milk; a libation
more: n. m. A hollow tree inserted in the mouth of a well in the Tarāī to prevent its falling in.

n. f. S. धारा 1. Edge (of a sword, etc); sharpness. 1. chhurī, chāqū, qaiṅchī, qalam-tarāsh kā dhār! Knife grinders' cry. 2. Talvār kī dhār bāṅdh dī . 3. Dhār par inhīṅ ko rakkho . 2. Stream; current. 1. Gaṅgā jī kī dhārā, pāp kāṭne ko ārā . Bhajan. The goddess Ganges' stream the sin-cleaver doth you redeem. 2. Dūdh, batāse haiṅ Jamnā kī ḍhār ko! Dehli street cry. Milk and sweets ho for offerings to Jamna! 3. Milk. Gāë ne dhār nahīṅ dī . 4. A libation. Dūdh batāshe kī dhār! dhār pe mārnā , Slang. v. a. To disregard; scorn; not to care for. Abrū woh tez, māre kaṭārī ko dhār par. dhār chaṛhānā yā denā , v. n. To pour a libation of milk, sugar, etc., and sometimes of liquor. He Gaṅgā māī! merā beṭā ā jāe to savā ser dūdh kī dhār chaṛhāūṅ! Hin. Wom. O Ganges mother! if my son come back to me a quarter over a seer of milk I'll offering make! dhār-dār , adj. Sharp. Dhārdār hatyār. dhār-dār , n…

n. f. S. धारा a multitude. 1. A band; banditti; an overwhelming multitude or crowd of robbers, locusts, etc. 2. An onset. Tane dhāṛ ke de dāṅ . Rus. I'll give you up to the bandits. (Said by the angry driver to his cattle.). dhāṛ paṛnā , v. n. To be attacked by a band of robbers, etc. dhāṛ mārnā , v. a. 1. To plunder in bands. 2. To plunder a crowd. dhāṛ'ī , n. m. One of a band of robbers.
s. f. 1. A line, lineament. 2. Stream, current. 3. Edge (of a sword, &c.). 4. Sharpness. دهارمارنا dhār mārnā, or دهارپرمارنا dhār par mārnā, To contemn, to despise.
subs: s.f. An even or general distribution of charges, rates, &c.
(or pe) mārnā, v.t. To disregard, contemn, despise, scorn (cf. dhār-mārnā)
adj. & s.m. Edged, sharp; — an edged or sharp tool or weapon
or dhār-dhūrā, s.m. Boundary (between two villages); boundary formed by a stream
(-par, or -kī), To give an edge (to), to sharpen; — to winnow
To give a stream of milk, to give milk
v.n. The edge to be turned or to become jagged
(-kī), To receive, or to drink, milk as it streams from the teat
(-par), To pass urine, to make water (on); to despise, scorn, &c. (see dhār-par māṛnā)
(-kī), To milk; — to produce an edge (on), to sharpen.

def: s.f. A line of descending fluid, a stream, current (of water, or of a river, &c.), a channel, a water-course; a torrent; a flood; a hard shower (of rain); a drop; — margin or border (of anything); circumference or periphery (of a wheel); — sharp edge (of any tool or weapon); — a multitude
more: n. f. A water-course; channel; current ( dhār ). dhārā-dār , ( Bot .) Channel-leaved.
s. f. A stream, a current.
subs: adv. In a stream, in streams
(˚rā+ās˚), s.m. A heavy shower; a large drop (of rain)

def: s.m. A body, band, party, side, faction; — a weight (usually of ten ser), or the quantity weighed by it; a counter-balancing weight; a large weight made up by counter-balancing stones, &c. against a small weight, and adding them to that weight, and so on, till the original weight is doubled, or quadrupled, or any required standard of weight is obtained
more: s. m. 1. A party. 2. A weight. دهڙا باندهنا dhaṛā bāndhnā, To make up a standard to weigh by, with bricks or any thing else; as, by having one weight (of a pound, suppose) it is doubled, quadrupled, and so on, till the weight requisite is obtained.
subs: (-kā), To weigh (by means of a dhaṛā)
v.n. To make up a party or side; — to make up a standard to weigh with (see dhaṛā)
or paṛnā, v.n. To be split up into bands, or parties, or factions
To frame or bring a counter charge.

r: dhāṛā, dhāṛ, dhāṛnā, dhāṛ paṛnā, par, dhāṛ denā, dhāṛ mārnā
def: s.m. = H دهاڙ धाड़ dhāṛ, s.f. (fr. dhāṛnā, q.v.), A multitude, crowd, band, gang; a band or gang of robbers, banditti; — a robbery; — a stream or body of water falling from a height, a torrent, a water-fall
more: s. m. A crowd, a multitude.
subs: (-par), A band of robbers to fall (upon), to be ataacked by a band of robbers
dhāṛ mārnā, To plunder in bands.

def: s.f. The earth
more: n. m. 1. (S. धट a balance.) Balancing a weight. 2. ( dhaṛ ) A party; side; faction. dhaṛā uṭhānā , v. n. To weigh ( tolnā , 1). dhaṛā aur kardā , n. m. Tare and tret. dhaṛā bāṅdhnā , v. n. To make up a side or party. dhaṛā k. , v. n. To balance; equipoise. Pahle dhaṛā kar lo. dhaṛe baṅdhnā, yā paṛnā , v. n. To be broken up or divided into factions or parties. Birādrī meṅ dhaṛe paṛ rahe haiṅ. ulṭā dhaṛā bāṅdhnā , v. a. To bring a countercharge. ṭake dhaṛā lagānā , v. n. lit . to sell at five seers the ṭakā . To sell off, or sell cheap.
subs: s.m. Earth, ground; surface, superficies, table
s.m. 'God of the earth,' an epithet of a Brāhman.

def: adv. & s.f. = H دهڙادهڙي धड़ाधड़ी dhaṛā-dhaṛī, s.f. See s.v. dhaṛ.
s.f. = H دهڙادهڙ धड़ाधड़ dharā-dhaṛ, adv. & s.f. See s.v. dhaṛ.

ety: dharā, i.q. dharāʼo, q.v.+har = Prk. घरं = S. गृहं
def: s.m. A depository.
s.f. Placing, putting down; holding, laying hold of, seizing.
adj. & s.m. Holding, containing; — what or who holds, a receptacle; a debtor.
s.f. Beating, throbbing, palpitating; beat, throb, palpitation; trepidation, fear, alarm (cf. thalak).
s.m. A loud report, explosion, crash, peal, raging (of a tempest), violence (of an attack, &c.), din and roar (of a battle); vehemence; rapidity; boldness
more: s. m. A crash, report of a gun, explosion.
subs: adv. With a loud report, with a crash; boldly, fearlessly; rapidly, quickly.

def: n. m. dhaṛā dhaṛ, dhaṛā-dhaṛī , n. f. A crash; report of a gun; explosion. 1. Jhaṛyoṅ ne is tarah kā dīyā ā-ke jhaṛ lagā, Sunye jidhar udhar ko dhaṛāke kī hai sadā . Nazīr. 2. Barso Rām dhaṛāke se, tūmbā bhar de āṭe se! Mendicants. O Rām! send rain in torrents down, my calabash with wheat meal fill! dhaṛāke se , adv. 1. With a crash. 2. Quickly. 3. Boldly. Dhaṛāke se yeh kām bhī kar do!
s.m. the Urdū form of the next, q.v.
s.m. A scarecrow ( = ḍhaṛkhā, q.v.). s.m. Trepidation, alarm ( = dhaṛkā); — a scarecrow ( = dhaṛak-kā, q.v.).
s.m. Report, explosion, crash ( = dhaṛākā, q.v.); sound of hammering, &c.; alarming, frightening, intimidating, bullying; bravado (cf. dharsh); — a crowd ( = dhaṛallā; bhīṛ).
v.n. = dhaṛaknā, q.v.
more: v. n. To palpitate; throb; beat.

ety: dhaṛak˚ = Prk. दड्ढक्क(इ), fr. S. दग्ध(p.p. of rt. दह्)+कृ
def: v.n. To blaze up, to burn fiercely; to be intensely hot (as the body in fever, &c.); to be inflamed (with anger, or lust, &c.; cf. bhaṛaknā); — to be distressed, to tremble (from fear, &c.); to beat, throb, palpitate, flutter.
more: v. n. To palpitate. To blaze(?).

def: v.t. = ḍhalaknā, q.v. v.n. To run down, to flow; to be poured out, be spilt, be shed; to fall or hang down loosely, be flaccid or loose (cf. laṭaknā); to decline, sink, fade ( = ḍhalnā); to rock; to roll, slide ( = ḍhulaknā)
s. (dakh.) A crashing noise ( Harris ).
s.m. Sound of beating or hammering, &c. ( = dhaṛakkā, q.v.); — a crowd, band, host, swarm ( = bhīṛ); — a scarecrow.
more: s. m. 1. The sound of hammering, &c. 2. Frightening, alarming, bullying, bravado. 3. A crowd.

def: n. m. H. dhāṛ . a crowd. A band; swarm ( bhīṛ ). Dhaṛalle ke dhaṛalle chale āte haiṅ .
n. The sound of a heavy body falling into water, or on soft ground. Pānī meṅ dhaṛām se kūd paṛā .
lawfully wedded wife (literally, “married in accordance to one's dharma”)
adv. With a crash, &c.

s.f. The earth ( = dharanī); the womb; the navel, the umbilical cord or vein (supposed by Hindū physicians to become occasionally displaced, and thus to give rise to various morbid symptoms); — a beam; — accent, tone
subs: dharan ḍignā or haṭnā, The womb, or the navel, to be displaced.

def: s.m. Holding, bearing, carrying, keeping, wearing; upholding, maintaining, sustaining, preserving; possessing, having, assuming; knowing, acquiring; undertaking
more: ( In comp ). S. धा to hold, bear. Holding; bearing. dhāran k., dhārnā , v. n. 1. To assume; adopt; take on oneself; bear. Phr. To commit oneself to a course. 2. To put on; wear. Janeo dhāran kīyā. dhār'nā , v. n. To pour (water). dūsrā mat dhāran k , v. n. To embrace a different faith.
verbal n. s. Holding, bearing, sustaining, upholding, assuming, keeping. See دهارنا dhārnā.
subs: v.t. To bear; to possess; to wear; to assume; to seize, retain; to draw, to absorb, &c.

def: s.m. Holding, seizing; having, possessing, keeping; bearing, supporting; placing; fastening, &c. (see dharnā); — a support; a fastening; a bank, dike, dam; — the female breast; — a sort of measure or weight.
more: n. f. S. धरण 1. The earth. 2. The womb. 3. A beam. 4. Accent; tone. dharan ḍignā, haṭnā, yā ṭalnā , v. n. To be moved (the womb or the navel from its place). dharan laṛānā yā milānā , Slang. v. a. To come in contact (in sexual intercourse).

s. f. 1. ( धरण ) A beam. 2. Accent, tone. 3. The navel (or rather the umbilical vein, perhaps the aorta or cœliack artery, as it is said to pulsate) which is supposed by the Hindū physicians to be occasionally removed from its place, and thus to occasion various morbid symptoms. This is called دهرن ڐگني dharan-ḍignī or ناف ٿلنا يا اکهڙنا nāf-ṭalnā or -ukhaṛnā, and the cure is attempted by friction of the belly. 4. See دهرنا dharnā.
s. m. Placing, putting, &c.: see دهرنا dharnā.

def: s.f. Keeping in remembrance, retaining in the mind; a good memory, memory; — continuance in rectitude, keeping in the right way, fortitude, firmness, steadiness, resolution; aptitude to acquire (wisdom, &c.).
v.t. To owe ( = dhārnā); — to deposit, to give in deposit ( = dharnā); — v.n. (prov.) To be laid hold of, be seized, be appre hended, be imprisoned.
more: v. a. To owe. Maiṅ kuchh tumhārā dharātā hūṅ!

def: s.m. Body and limbs; — (dialec.) adj. Large, lusty, stalwart, strapping
subs: 'naked in body and limb,' stark naked).

def: s. (dakh.) Placer, placing.
s.f. The earth, globe; earth, soil, land, ground; — a beam
subs: s.m. lit. 'Bearing or sustaining the earth'; an epithet of Śesh-nāg, the serpent which is said to uphold the earth; — an epithet of Vishnu in the form of a tortoise and a boar; — a mountain
s.f. 'Daughter of the earth,' an epithet of Sītā wife of Rāma (as produced from the soil by Janaka when he was ploughing a spot to prepare it for sacrifice)
s.m. An esculent root or bulb.
